subcodes.title: Words spoken ca. 1900
subcodes.first_line: The material men of this world are like poison to the spiritual. They are the calamities and inconveniences which a man meets in the worldly life…. The people of God have no dependence upon the conditions of this world; they neither become embittered with the bitterness of the cup
subcodes.subjects: @characteristics and conduct of true believers, @acquiescence and resignation; contentment, @fellowship with the wayward and ungodly, @spiritual emotions and susceptibilities, @transience; worthlessness of the physical world, @detachment; severance; renunciation; patience, @tests and trials; sacrifice and suffering, @worldliness vs. other-worldliness, @moderation; frugality; simplicity
